REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (61 Marview Way, San Francisco)
Off Calendar Apr-19-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for and with Elizabeth Adler.

The sale was confirmed at $678,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $131,000.00 is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

Petition
Off Calendar Jun-22-2006 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Elizabeth Adler, Petitioner; Lawrence Siracusa, Counsel for the Petitioner.

These matters were heard together: case #288578 and #288604. Nancy Cowhig's appearances was excused as she did not wish to appear.

Counsel noted that the Conservatee's daughter was present in court. The Court noted that the matters were procedurally in order and that the conditions fo Probate Code Sections 811 were met and that the Conservatorship was appropriate and appointed Elizabeth Adler Conservator of the Person and Estate of Nancy Cowhig (signing an Order in open court). Elizabeth Adler was also appointed Successor Trustee of the Nancy Cowhig Trust.

Further Orders:

Inventory, General Plan, and Assessment must be filed two weeks before: October 3, 2006.
First Account and Status Report must be filed two weeks before: August 28, 2007.
If the estate includes real property, proof of recording of letters must be filed two weeks before: October 3, 2006.
Assessment fee of $690.00 is charged.
Bond for the trust was set at $300,000; No bond was set for the Conservatorship.

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy L. McMath; Reporter: Helen Soret-Matley CSR 225; Court Investigator: Melinda Gean; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: Candace Lum.

Petition
Off Calendar Jun-01-2006 Continued To Jun-22-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Schultz (Counsel for the Conservator).

Mr. Schultz appeared for the purpose of continuing the hearing on the matter to June 22, 2006 and extending the Temporary Letters to that date (which Order for same was presented and signed in open court). Mr. Schultz also noted that the matter of the Conservatee's Trust (Case No. 288604) was before the court and had been inadvently miscalendared by the Court for the 1:30 p.m. calendar. Noting the same, the Court continued the hearing on the Trust to June 22, 2006 as well and extended the Order appointing the Temporary Successor Trustee to that date (Order for same signed in open court).

Judicial Officer: Commissioner Dorothy McMath; Reporter: Ron St. Clair CSR# 1170; Court Investigator: Jeanine Lim; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles.
